danger_signals.visual_form: deteriorating ride appearance, broken track segments, sparks or visual damage
danger_signals.meaning: unsafe rides with high crash/injury risk; guest safety compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: guest sprites showing distress animations, vomit effects, crying indicators
danger_signals.meaning: park conditions degrading; low morale indicates potential guest exodus or vandalism

danger_signals.visual_form: red text/indicators in budget window, loan warnings, foreclosure notifications
danger_signals.meaning: park bankruptcy imminent; economic collapse of management strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: excessive guest clustering, pathways completely filled, movement congestion
danger_signals.meaning: infrastructure overwhelmed; disease spread risk, guest dissatisfaction escalating

danger_signals.visual_form: graffiti on structures, litter accumulation, property damage sprites
danger_signals.meaning: low park cleanliness/maintenance; social disorder increasing

safety_signals.visual_form: clean, intact track; regular maintenance crew visible; ride in active operation
guest sprites showing happy animations, purchase interactions, riding attractions
safety_signals.meaning: park morale healthy; satisfactory world-state conditions

safety_signals.visual_form: green text in budget display, positive cash flow indicators, loan repayment progress
safety_signals.meaning: economic safety; park is solvent and sustainable

safety_signals.visual_form: litter bins present, janitors visible, pathways clear of debris
safety_signals.meaning: proper maintenance infrastructure; hygiene standards met

safety_signals.visual_form: maintenance workers, security guards, janitors distributed throughout park

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: player avatar; park name/logo in top UI
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: owned structures, player-placed paths, custom ride designs with park branding

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: generic humanoid sprites with distinct animations per mood
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: angry/vandal variants show aggressive animations; drunk/sick guests display distress
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: concentrated in attractions, on pathways, clustered near food/bathroom facilities

faction_indicators.color_identity: uniform colors by role (maintenance yellow, security dark, janitors light)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: understaffed/overworked staff show slower movement, lower efficiency
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: maintenance workers at ride stations, janitors on pathways, security patrolling

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: inspector avatars appearing for safety audits and ride inspections
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: appear during construction/ride testing; red X marks failed inspections

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: expanded park layout, new attractions under construction, crowded pathways, premium amenities visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: guests spending freely, staff working efficiently, expansion projects proceeding

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: construction halted, ride closures appearing, red budget warnings, loan notices
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: staff layoffs reduce visual worker presence, guest count declines, vandalism increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: broken ride assets, warning signs, police/inspector presence, crash animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: guests fleeing attractions, staff emergency responses, park evacuation procedures triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pathways completely filled with guest sprites, queues extending far, litter scattered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: slow guest movement, reduced ride access, increased bathroom/food facility strain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: weather effects (rain, snow), seasonal decorations, guest clothing variations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: guest count fluctuation, ride popularity changes, seasonal profit/loss cycles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: deteriorating ride appearance, missing maintenance crew sprites, graffiti accumulation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increasing ride breakdowns, guest complaints rising, accident rates increasing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pristine structures, visible maintenance/cleaning staff, happy guest concentration, efficient queues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: minimal guest complaints, high attraction capacity, staff productivity optimal

environmental_storytelling.example: aging rides show visual deterioration (rust textures, broken segments) telling story of neglect; maintenance crew presence/absence communicates investment level
crowded areas with long queues visually communicate popular attractions vs. empty underutilized rides, revealing guest preference patterns
janitor and maintenance sprites visible/absent communicate resource allocation; their work visible in pathway cleanliness and ride functionality
budget window colors (green/red), loan amounts, cash flow display communicate economic story without text
weather visual changes and seasonal decorations create temporal world-state narrative; guest clothing variants reinforce seasonal context
terraforming, pathways, and custom layouts visually communicate player strategic choices and park evolution over time
graffiti, litter, and broken benches visually signal social degradation and low park morale without explicit text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: walking purposefully toward attractions, purchasing items, riding continuously, happy animation loops
slow wandering, standing idle, vomit/distress animations, leaving park/exiting queues
staggering movement, distress indicators, potential vandalism behaviors
regular patrol of rides, visible repairs, maintaining ride safety status
slower movement, incomplete repairs, rides falling into disrepair despite staff presence
regular sweeping on pathways, litter removal, park cleanliness maintained
sparse pathways, litter accumulation visible, overcrowding litter effects unmanaged
patrolling park areas, preventing vandalism, managing guest disruptions
unchecked vandalism, guest fights, lack of order maintenance
pristine appearance, full operational capacity, high guest throughput
visual deterioration appearing, occasional breakdowns, reduced guest confidence
broken segments visible, frequent crashes, guest injury/death events, closure forced

notes: RollerCoaster Tycoon is a management simulation without explicit factions in conflict; instead, world-state manifestation occurs through guest satisfaction, financial indicators, and park infrastructure quality. The game uses visual metaphor extensively: guest behavior sprites communicate park health, staff presence communicates resource allocation, and infrastructure decay communicates time/neglect. The manifestation layer relies heavily on density visualization (crowding), visual asset degradation (rust/breaks), and behavioral animation loops rather than explicit danger/safety symbols. Political situation emerges implicitly through economic pressure (loans, bankruptcy) and regulatory inspection events. The two-fires paradigm maps: player vs. park-system (emergent constraints) rather than player vs. faction opponents.
